_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Marion County, TX. Record survival and courthouse history for the county of Jefferson — the antebellum steamboat port. Records date from 1860 county organization.

previous_courthouses.fate: Marion County was created in 1860, carved from Cass County. Early records transferred or newly created. No significant fire damage.

record_losses.description: Marion County's courthouse has not experienced a major fire. Records begin from county creation in 1860. For records predating 1860, researchers must check Cass County records, as Marion was carved from Cass County. Jefferson's steamboat-era commercial records — shipping manifests, warehouse receipts — are separate from the courthouse and partially preserved at the Jefferson Historical Museum.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} was in the Jefferson area before 1860, records are in Cass County's files. After 1860, Marion County records are intact. The historical museum in Jefferson holds commercial and steamboat-era records not available at the courthouse.
record_losses.source: Texas State Library and Archives Commission. County Records Inventory.

deeds.completeness: Good from county creation. For pre-1860 deeds, check Cass County.

vital_records.repository: Texas Department of State Health Services

substitute_sources.source_type: Jefferson Historical Museum and Excelsior House archives
substitute_sources.description: The Jefferson Historical Museum and the Excelsior House hotel hold collections related to Jefferson's steamboat era — shipping records, hotel registers, commercial correspondence, and photographs.
substitute_sources.repository: Jefferson Historical Museum, Jefferson, TX; Excelsior House archives
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Steamboat-era hotel registers and shipping manifests can document individuals passing through Jefferson — particularly useful for families arriving in East Texas via the water route from New Orleans and Shreveport.

substitute_sources.description: Marion County was created from Cass County in 1860. Records for the Jefferson area before that date are in Cass County's files at the Linden courthouse.
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Essential for pre-1860 research in the Jefferson area. Deeds, marriages, and court records filed before Marion County's creation are in Cass County.
substitute_sources.source: Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

narrative_hooks: Marion County was carved from Cass County in 1860 — if {ancestor_name} was in Jefferson before that date, the records are at the Cass County courthouse in Linden, not in Jefferson.

The Jefferson Historical Museum holds steamboat-era records that the courthouse does not — shipping manifests and hotel registers that may document {ancestor_name}'s arrival in East Texas by water from New Orleans.

Marion County's records start in 1860 — the year the county was created, the year before the Civil War, and the beginning of a new record series for a town that was already Texas's second-largest city.
Texas vital records start in 1903 — for {ancestor_name}'s birth in Marion County before that date, census records and church records are the primary sources.
source_references: Texas State Library and Archives Commission. County Records Inventory.
Works Progress Administration. Historical Records Survey: Texas County Records. Austin, 1937–1942.
